# Data Quality Injection Plan
**Project:** PointX — Subscription Revenue Intelligence
**File affected:** `data/raw/pointx_raw_dataset.csv`
**Generated by:** `scripts/01_generate_raw_dataset.py`

---

## Purpose

This document describes every intentional data quality issue injected
into the raw dataset. It serves three purposes:

1. **Transparency** — documents what was injected and why
2. **Cleaning validation** — provides a ground truth to verify
   that cleaning steps resolved each issue correctly
3. **Skill demonstration** — shows deliberate design of realistic
   data quality challenges across multiple issue categories

---

## Injection Summary

| # | Issue Type | Field(s) Affected | Injection Rate | Cleaning Action Required |
|---|-----------|-------------------|---------------|--------------------------|
| 1 | Null values | `mrr`, `transaction_count` | ~2% of active records each | Impute or remove nulls |
| 2 | Logical inconsistency | `active_status`, `mrr` | ~2% of records per case | Align active_status with mrr > 0 rule |
| 3 | MRR component conflict | `new_mrr`, `expansion_mrr` | ~5% of new_mrr > 0 records | Zero out expansion_mrr where new_mrr > 0 |
| 4 | Numeric outliers | `total_payment_volume`, `customer_acquisition_cost` | ~2% of records each | Cap or flag extreme values |
| 5 | Duplicate records | `merchant_id` + `month` | ~1% of records duplicated | Deduplicate on composite key |
| 6 | Text case inconsistency | `merchant_governorate` | ~2% of records | Normalize to title case |
| 7 | Typo / misspelling | `merchant_governorate` | ~2% of records | Standardize to valid governorate list |
| 8 | Leading/trailing whitespace | `merchant_governorate` | ~2% of records | Strip whitespace |
| 9 | Data type error | `customer_acquisition_cost` | ~2% of records | Cast to numeric, extract value |
| 10 | Impossible negative value | `customer_acquisition_cost` | ~2% of records | Flag and remove or replace |
| 11 | Date format inconsistency | `month` | ~2% of records | Standardize to YYYY-MM-DD |

---

## Detailed Injection Logic

### Issue 1 — Null Values
**Fields:** `mrr`, `transaction_count`
**Scope:** Active records only (`active_status = True`)
**Logic:** Randomly null 2% of active records per field independently
**Why realistic:** Sensor failures, ETL pipeline gaps, and partial
syncs are common sources of nulls in transactional systems
**Cleaning action:** Investigate nulls in context — active merchants
with null MRR may warrant imputation from prior month or removal

---

### Issue 2 — Logical Inconsistencies (active_status vs mrr)
**Fields:** `active_status`, `mrr`
**Two cases injected:**
- Case A: `active_status = True` but `mrr = 0` (~2% of active records)
- Case B: `active_status = False` but `mrr > 0` (~2% of inactive records)

**Why realistic:** Status flags and revenue fields often update
asynchronously in billing systems — a merchant marked active before
their first payment, or marked inactive before revenue is zeroed out
**Cleaning action:** Apply business rule: `active_status` must equal
`mrr > 0`. Flag conflicts for review before overwriting

---

### Issue 3 — MRR Component Conflicts
**Fields:** `new_mrr`, `expansion_mrr`
**Logic:** 5% of records where `new_mrr > 0` also receive a non-zero
`expansion_mrr`
**Why realistic:** CRM and billing system misclassification of
upgrade events in the onboarding month
**Business rule violated:** A merchant cannot be new and expanding
in the same month — `new_mrr` and `expansion_mrr` are mutually
exclusive per MRR decomposition logic
**Cleaning action:** Where `new_mrr > 0`, set `expansion_mrr = 0`

---

### Issue 4 — Numeric Outliers
**Fields:** `total_payment_volume`, `customer_acquisition_cost`
**Logic:**
- TPV: 2% of active records multiplied by 100 (extreme high values)
- CAC: 2% of records multiplied by 100 (extreme high values)

**Why realistic:** Data entry errors, unit mismatches (fils vs JOD),
and system migration artifacts are common outlier sources
**Cleaning action:** Apply IQR or z-score method to detect and cap
or flag outliers per field

---

### Issue 5 — Duplicate Records
**Fields:** `merchant_id` + `month` (composite key)
**Logic:** 1% of records duplicated and shuffled into dataset
**Why realistic:** ETL pipeline re-runs, double-inserts, and
merge conflicts are the most common sources of duplicates
in operational data warehouses
**Cleaning action:** Deduplicate on `(merchant_id, month)` composite
key, keeping first occurrence

---

### Issue 6 — Text Case Inconsistency
**Field:** `merchant_governorate`
**Injected value:** `"amman"` (lowercase) into ~2% of records
**Why realistic:** Manual data entry from multiple sources with
no input validation
**Cleaning action:** Apply `.str.title()` or `.str.strip().str.title()`
then validate against the governorate reference list

---

### Issue 7 — Typo / Misspelling
**Field:** `merchant_governorate`
**Injected value:** `"Zaqra"` (misspelling of "Zarqa") into ~2% of records
**Why realistic:** Manual entry errors in registration forms or
CRM data imports
**Cleaning action:** Use fuzzy matching (e.g. `rapidfuzz`) or a
lookup table to map to the closest valid governorate name

---

### Issue 8 — Leading/Trailing Whitespace
**Field:** `merchant_governorate`
**Injected value:** `" Amman "` (padded with spaces) into ~2% of records
**Why realistic:** Copy-paste artifacts from spreadsheets and
web form submissions
**Cleaning action:** Apply `.str.strip()` before any categorical
validation or grouping

---

### Issue 9 — Data Type Error (string in numeric field)
**Field:** `customer_acquisition_cost`
**Injected value:** `"50JOD"` (numeric value with currency suffix
as string) into ~2% of records
**Why realistic:** Currency fields exported from legacy systems
or Excel files often carry unit suffixes
**Cleaning action:** Extract numeric component using regex
`str.extract(r'([\d.]+)')` then cast to float

---

### Issue 10 — Impossible Negative Value
**Field:** `customer_acquisition_cost`
**Injected value:** `-20` into ~2% of records
**Why realistic:** Credit adjustments or system errors can produce
negative values in cost fields
**Business rule violated:** CAC must be ≥ 0 per data dictionary
**Cleaning action:** Flag negative values — replace with null
for investigation rather than dropping silently

---

### Issue 11 — Date Format Inconsistency
**Field:** `month`
**Injected value:** `YYYY/MM/DD` format (slash separator) replacing
standard `YYYY-MM-DD` in ~2% of records
**Why realistic:** Excel, legacy ERP systems, and regional settings
produce mixed date formats in exported files
**Cleaning action:** Parse all date values with `pd.to_datetime()`
using `infer_datetime_format=True` or explicit format handling,
then standardize to `YYYY-MM-DD`

---

## Validation Checklist (Post-Cleaning)

Use this checklist to verify your cleaning script resolved every issue:

- [ ] `mrr` has zero nulls in active records
- [ ] `transaction_count` has zero nulls in active records
- [ ] No record where `active_status = True` and `mrr = 0`
- [ ] No record where `active_status = False` and `mrr > 0`
- [ ] No record where `new_mrr > 0` and `expansion_mrr > 0` simultaneously
- [ ] No `total_payment_volume` values exceeding [your defined cap]
- [ ] No `customer_acquisition_cost` values exceeding [your defined cap]
- [ ] Zero duplicate `(merchant_id, month)` pairs
- [ ] All `merchant_governorate` values match the 13-governorate reference list
- [ ] `customer_acquisition_cost` column is fully numeric (float dtype)
- [ ] No negative `customer_acquisition_cost` values
- [ ] All `month` values parse correctly as dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format

---

## Reference — Valid Governorate List
```python
VALID_GOVERNORATES = [
    "Amman", "Zarqa", "Irbid", "Aqaba", "Madaba",
    "Jerash", "Ajloun", "Mafraq", "Balqa", "Karak",
    "Tafilah", "Ma'an", "Petra"
]
```
